title: PhantomData lifetime on CryptoCbGuard prevents key handle use-after-free — keep it
description: CryptoCbGuard<'a> holds PhantomData<&'a mut Client> so the borrow checker prevents dropping Client while the guard is alive. The wolfCrypt CryptoCb global table stores a raw *mut whClientContext; without this lifetime, a user could drop Client and then trigger a CryptoCb operation via wolfcrypt-rs, producing a dangling pointer dereference. A future contributor might suggest simplifying to CryptoCbGuard() (no lifetime), citing that the raw pointer approach 'works in practice'. Reject: the lifetime is the ONLY thing making the safety invariant compile-time enforced.
